title: chore: remove dead image params (negative_prompt, enhance, nologo)
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Removes three dead/no-op image-API params, one commit per concern:

- **`negative_prompt`** (#11506) — dead end-to-end: flux moved to Fireworks (doesn't use it) and the zimage backend servers ignore the field.
- **`enhance`** (#11368) — a no-op flag: nothing in the gen worker reads the parsed value to rewrite the prompt, and the per-model `enhance` capability flag in `models.ts` had no consumer.
- **`nologo`** (#9929) — never declared in either gen image schema, so Zod already strips it before any backend sees it; pure stale plumbing.

Surface touched: gen schemas/params/routes/models + self-hosted forward, SDK (client/types/README), MCP tool schemas + forwarders, polli-cli flags (image + video), the Play UI checkbox + copy, the 4 image/video test fixtures, and first-party scripts/docs (healthcheck rotation scripts, social helper, monitor skill, z-image example).

Both gen schemas use `z.object()`, so unknown keys are stripped — existing requests sending these params are silently ignored (no rejection, no grace period needed).

`APIDOCS.md` is auto-generated from the deployed schema and regenerates post-deploy, so it is intentionally not hand-edited here.

Verified: gen / SDK / frontend / polli-cli typecheck, biome, and gen image + docs + index tests all pass.

Fixes #11506
Fixes #11368
Fixes #9929

gist.headline: Trimming the dead leaves
gist.blurb: We've swept away some dusty, unused image parameters like negative_prompt and enhance to keep our API schemas clean and snappy.
gist.summary: Removed the deprecated negative_prompt, enhance, and nologo parameters from the image API, SDK, MCP, and CLI since they were no longer functional.
gist.impact: Developers will see a cleaner API surface and SDK types, while existing requests sending these old parameters will continue to be safely ignored.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Nomnom creature cleaning up a digital garden. Nomnom eating old, withered code-leaves representing the dead parameters.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan Nomnom creature happily eating dusty, withered code-leaves labeled 'enhance' and 'nologo' in a digital garden. The cute bee mascot wearing a tiny bandana sweeps the path with a small broom. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
